nativeRange.habitat: Hobby cultivar of the Bucephalandra genus, which is endemic to the island of Borneo, where wild populations grow as rheophytes attached to rocks and submerged wood along the banks and beds of shaded, fast-flowing rainforest streams

decorPreferences: lava rock, driftwood or other porous hardscape for the rhizome to attach to
shaded or moderately lit placement; tends to thrive in shaded areas of the tank
gentle to moderate flow across the rhizome to mimic rheophyte stream conditions
stable, established tank — generally advised against introducing into newly cycled systems

goodWith: Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp, which graze biofilm without harming the plant
other low-light epiphytes such as Anubias, Java fern, mosses and other Bucephalandra cultivars
avoidWith: large herbivorous or rhizome-disturbing fish such as goldfish, silver dollars and large plecos
vigorous diggers such as large cichlids that may dislodge attached rhizomes
newly set up tanks with unstable parameters where buce melt is more likely

summary: Bucephalandra 'Velvet' is a hobby cultivar prized for its distinctive soft, velvety-textured leaves that shift from light brown-red when young to a deep brownish-green at maturity. Care matches the genus — attach the rhizome to hardscape, never bury it, and provide shaded low-to-medium light.
careNotes: Bucephalandra 'Velvet' is a hobby cultivar of the Borneo-endemic Bucephalandra genus (family Araceae) and shares the same husbandry as the parent genus entry (fw-plant-016): treat it as an epiphytic rheophyte attached to lava rock, driftwood or other porous hardscape with super glue gel, sewing thread or fishing line, and never bury the rhizome. The defining trait of this cultivar is leaf texture: mature foliage is consistently described as soft and velvety, which distinguishes it visually from the glossy or iridescent finishes of cultivars like 'Black Pearl' or 'Brownie'. New leaves are commonly reported to emerge with a light brown-red tone and darken to a deep brownish-green at maturity, with some bluish-purple highlights under good light. Water parameters are generally advised around 21-28 °C and pH ~6.0-7.5 (Modern Aquarium gives 70-82°F / ≈21-28 °C; Buceplant cites a broader pH 6.0-8.0 range, which has been averaged toward the narrower hobby-consensus 6.0-7.5 used for the genus). Lighting is generally advised as low to medium; the cultivar is reported to thrive in shaded placements and may scorch or attract algae under high light without CO2. CO2 is generally considered optional but tends to produce faster, more compact growth and richer coloration; regular light fertilization is commonly considered beneficial. Sold sizes are small (Modern Aquarium notes 1-2 inch stem lengths at sale, ≈2.5-5 cm), and the cultivar is reported to remain on the smaller end of the genus over time — typically forming a low cluster in the 5-15 cm range. Like other bucephalandras it is prone to 'buce melt' when first transitioning from emersed nursery growth to submersed conditions or after sudden parameter changes; as long as the rhizome stays firm, recovery is generally reported over the following weeks. Propagate by dividing the rhizome with sharp scissors and reattaching divisions to fresh hardscape. See the genus entry (fw-plant-016) for shared husbandry details.
breedingNotes: Standard hobby propagation is by rhizome division: cut the rhizome at a natural bend with clean, sharp scissors, leaving each division with several leaves and visible roots, and reattach the new piece to a separate piece of hardscape using super glue gel, sewing thread or fishing line. Growth is slow, so divisions typically need several weeks before putting out new leaves.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirms Borneo shaded-stream origin, temperature 70-82°F (≈21-28 °C), low-to-moderate lighting, slow growth, foreground-to-midground placement, hardy care level, soft velvety leaf texture, and color range from deep green to blue and purple hues under good light; reports 1-2 inch stem lengths at sale.

additional.name: Buceplant
additional.notes: Cross-checked: Borneo endemism, pH 6.0-8.0 (averaged with primary toward the genus 6.0-7.5 consensus), low-to-medium lighting with a preference for shaded placement, slow growth, optional CO2 (recommended for faster growth), foreground-to-midground placement, must-not-be-buried epiphytic attachment to rock or driftwood, propagation by rhizome division, easy care level, and the characteristic light-brown-red young leaves maturing to deep brownish-green with a soft velvety texture.

additional.name: Bucephalandra (genus parent entry fw-plant-016)
additional.notes: Cross-referenced shared genus husbandry (water parameters 21-28 °C / pH ~6-7.5, gH 3-15, low-to-medium lighting, optional CO2, slow growth, epiphytic placement, do-not-bury-rhizome rule, buce melt during emersed-to-submersed transition, propagation by rhizome division). This cultivar inherits the genus baseline.
